title: Tendery System - First Delivery Feedback Session
company_name: Hay Huevos (temporary name - AI software company)
summary: First delivery review of the Tendery quotation system for a logistics/transportation company. The team demoed the full quotation workflow including client selection from Odoo CRM, route configuration, pricing engine (CICETAC + RNDC + historical data), profitability/utility validation, PDF generation, email delivery, QR-based client approval, and quotation cloning. Key feedback centered on changing the pricing UI so users edit the final client price instead of margins, adding default minimum profitability per vehicle type, classifying routes as urban/national/container-return, and implementing an amendment feature for approved quotations. A 2-week user acceptance testing phase was agreed upon with a follow-up meeting in 8 days.

discussion_points.description: Extensive discussion on how profitability (%) and utility (absolute value) interact. Current system takes the nominally higher of the two, but both must be met or the quotation goes to approval. Team agreed to change UX: user should edit the final client price, and the system should auto-calculate and validate both margin checks (utility and profitability). If both pass, proceed; if either fails, require approval.

Vehicle types need corrections: remove weight labels to avoid confusion between sub-types (Minimula S2/S3, Tractomula variants). Missing vehicle type 'Poderosa' (intermediate between Minimula and Tractomula). Weight capacities need correction (NHR: 2t not 3.5t, Turbo: 4.5t, Sencillo: 5-8.5t). The invalid 'MULA' type in the Excel template needs to be fixed.

System needs a route type field to distinguish between urban, national, and container return operations. Urban routes don't have CICETAC regulation. This classification affects which cost sources are available and which profitability policies apply per vehicle type.

Three cost sources: CICETAC (regulated floor), RNDC (market data via statistical analysis/Gaussian curve), and Historical data. RNDC currently uses month-old data from January Excel. Goal is daily updates. Buenaventura route (80% of quotations) is most volatile and may need manual handling initially. Debate on using first standard deviation above vs. below the mean (85th percentile of dispatch volume recommended).

Most structurally significant change requested. Need ability to modify accepted quotations when operational conditions change (e.g., container return location changes from express to consolidated). Amendments should be permission-controlled. Applies especially to spot quotations where client instructions change after approval.

Quotations sent via email with PDF attachment and QR code. Client can accept, reject, or request advisor. Discussion on needing WhatsApp delivery and email-based one-click approval buttons since clients often approve informally via WhatsApp. Internal approval queue managed by Carlos and Juan David.

Complex scenarios like Buenaventura-Bogota with container return to Tocancipa or port consolidation. Resolved via SAT (additional services) for extra deliveries/returns. Container returns for different shipping lines (Evergreen, CMA) have different costs. ITR operations handled as SATs, not routes.
Client-facing prices must be rounded up to the next multiple of 10,000 COP. Currently not implemented - system shows exact calculated values instead of commercially rounded figures.

Future phase: approved quotations and tariffs should sync to Postar TMS so operations can create service orders and manifests directly. Currently, the quotation module operates independently. Service order creation from approved quotations is the next development phase.

Demonstrated ability to clone existing quotations for new clients. Clone brings routes but missing some configuration details. Need to fix clone button to carry full configuration. Useful for repeat quotations (e.g., same routes for different clients).

Current default is 15 days. Discussion on appropriate validity periods - weekly for volatile routes (Buenaventura) vs. annual for established tariff clients (like Textiles). Vigency periods should be configurable per quotation.

Current emails sent from 'huevos' domain which is unprofessional for client-facing communications. Need to configure proper company domain. Also need letterhead/branding on PDF quotations and user-specific email signatures.

action_items.description: Change pricing UI: user edits final client price, system auto-calculates and validates both profitability (%) and utility (absolute value) with visual check indicators
Add default minimum profitability and utility values per vehicle type that auto-populate but remain editable
Remove weight labels from vehicle type selector to avoid confusion between sub-types
Add route classification field: urban, national, container return
Implement price rounding to next superior multiple of 10,000 COP
Re-enable profitability policy alerts per vehicle type (temporarily disabled)
Send SAT (additional services) list with costs to development team
Implement quotation amendment/modification functionality for approved quotations with permission control
Change email sending domain from temporary 'huevos' domain to proper company domain
Add mobile-friendly approval via email link (one-click approve button in email)
Fix quotation cloning to carry full route configuration
Configure approval users: Carlos Cardona and Juan David as quotation approvers
Enable Excel upload for historical cost data until automated integration is available
Contact Ministry for updated RNDC data feed
Commercial team to test the Tendery system during 2-week UAT phase and document observations
Add letterhead/branding to PDF quotation documents
Add user-specific email signature to outgoing quotation emails

decisions: Follow-up meeting scheduled in 8 days (March 11, 2026) at the same time
2-week User Acceptance Testing (UAT) phase starts immediately - all commercial team members will test the system
Carlos Cardona and Juan David will be configured as quotation approvers
Pricing UI will be redesigned: user edits final client price, system validates both profitability and utility checks
Multi-delivery and container return scenarios will be handled via SAT (additional services), not as separate routes
Buenaventura route pricing will be managed manually initially until automated data feed is established
RNDC historical data will be updated monthly via Excel upload as baseline, with daily updates as the goal
Route classification (urban/national/container return) will be added as a field in route configuration
Default minimum margins should auto-populate per vehicle type but remain editable by commercial users
Quotation validity periods should be configurable (not fixed at 15 days) to support both spot and annual tariff clients
Amendment functionality for approved quotations is approved as a feature requirement (most structural change)
Phase 1 focuses on quotation module only; Phase 2 will integrate with Postar TMS for service orders
next_steps: Development team applies immediate UI adjustments (pricing input change, vehicle weights, route classification, rounding) before next meeting
Carlos sends SAT list with costs and contacts Ministry for RNDC data
Commercial team (Kelly, Paola, Marcela) begins testing the Tendery system this week
All approvals routed to Carlos Cardona during testing phase
Team reconvenes in 8 days with compiled observations and change requests
After quotation module is validated, begin Phase 2 planning: Postar TMS integration and service order creation

diagrams.title: Tendery Quotation Workflow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    A[Select Client from Odoo CRM] --> B[Configure Routes]
    B --> B1{Few routes?}
    B1 -->|Yes| B2[Manual Entry via UI]
    B1 -->|No| B3[Upload Excel Template]
    B2 --> C[Pricing Engine]
    B3 --> C
    C --> C1[Compare Cost Sources]
    C1 --> C2[CICETAC Floor]
    C1 --> C3[RNDC Statistical Analysis]
    C1 --> C4[Historical Data]
    C2 --> C5[Take Highest Cost Floor]
    C3 --> C5
    C4 --> C5
    C5 --> D[Apply Margin]
    D --> D1[User Sets Final Client Price]
    D1 --> D2{Both Checks Pass?}
    D2 -->|Utility OK AND Profitability OK| E[Add Additional Services - SAT]
    D2 -->|Either Fails| F[Route to Approval Queue]
    F --> F1[Approver Reviews]
    F1 -->|Approved| E
    F1 -->|Rejected| D1
    E --> G[Generate PDF Quotation]
    G --> H[Send to Client via Email]
    H --> I[Client Reviews via QR Code]
    I --> I1{Client Decision}
    I1 -->|Accept| J[Quotation Approved]
    I1 -->|Reject| K[Quotation Rejected]
    I1 -->|Request Advisor| L[Commercial Follow-up]
    L --> D1
    J --> M[Create Service Order - Phase 2]

diagrams.title: Pricing Engine - Cost Source Comparison
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Sources[Cost Sources]
        CICETAC[CICETAC\nRegulated Floor Price]
        RNDC[RNDC Optimal\nStatistical Analysis\nGaussian Curve]
        HIST[Historical Data\nFrom Postar TMS]
    end
    subgraph Analysis[Statistical Processing]
        RNDC --> GAUSS[Gaussian Distribution]
        GAUSS --> P85[85th Percentile\nof Dispatch Volume]
        P85 --> OPTIMAL[Optimal RNDC Value]
    end
    subgraph Comparison[Price Comparison]
        CICETAC --> MAX[Take Highest\nCost Floor]
        OPTIMAL --> MAX
        HIST --> MAX
    end
    subgraph Margin[Margin Application]
        MAX --> COST[Base Cost]
        COST --> FINAL[Final Client Price]
        FINAL --> CHECK1{Utility Check\nAbsolute Value}
        FINAL --> CHECK2{Profitability Check\nPercentage}
        CHECK1 -->|Pass| OK[Both Must Pass]
        CHECK2 -->|Pass| OK
        CHECK1 -->|Fail| APPROVAL[Approval Required]
        CHECK2 -->|Fail| APPROVAL
        OK --> ROUND[Round to Next\n10000 COP Multiple]
    end

diagrams.title: System Integration Architecture
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph External[External Systems]
        ODOO[Odoo CRM\nClient Database]
        CICETAC_SYS[CICETAC\nMinistry Regulation]
        RNDC_SYS[RNDC\nMarket Data]
        POSTAR[Postar TMS\nTransport Management]
    end
    subgraph Tendery[Tendery System - Phase 1]
        CLI[Client Selection] --> ROUTE[Route Configuration]
        ROUTE --> PRICING[Pricing Engine]
        PRICING --> QUOTE[Quotation Generator]
        QUOTE --> PDF[PDF Generator]
        QUOTE --> APPROVE[Approval Queue]
        QUOTE --> CLONE[Clone Quotation]
        PDF --> EMAIL[Email Service]
        EMAIL --> QR[QR Code Portal\nClient Accept/Reject]
    end
    subgraph Phase2[Phase 2 - Future]
        SS[Service Order\nCreation]
        MANIFEST[Manifest Generation]
        BILLING[Billing Integration]
    end
    ODOO -->|Client Data| CLI
    CICETAC_SYS -->|Floor Prices| PRICING
    RNDC_SYS -->|Market Prices\nExcel Monthly| PRICING
    POSTAR -->|Historical Costs| PRICING
    QUOTE -->|Approved Tariffs| SS
    SS --> POSTAR
    SS --> MANIFEST
    MANIFEST --> BILLING

diagrams.title: Quotation Approval and Client Interaction Flow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant COM as Commercial User
    participant TEN as Tendery System
    participant APR as Approver (Carlos/Juan David)
    participant CLI as Client
    
    COM->>TEN: Create Quotation with Routes
    TEN->>TEN: Calculate Pricing (CICETAC + RNDC + Historical)
    COM->>TEN: Set Final Client Price per Route
    TEN->>TEN: Validate Utility & Profitability
    
    alt Both Checks Pass
        TEN->>TEN: Mark as Ready to Send
    else Either Check Fails
        TEN->>APR: Send to Approval Queue
        APR->>TEN: Approve or Reject
    end
    
    TEN->>TEN: Generate PDF with Tariff Annex
    COM->>TEN: Send Quotation to Client
    TEN->>CLI: Email with PDF + QR Code
    
    alt Client Accepts
        CLI->>TEN: Accept via QR Portal
        TEN->>COM: Notification - Accepted
        Note over COM,TEN: Phase 2: Create Service Order
    else Client Rejects
        CLI->>TEN: Reject with Comments
        TEN->>COM: Notification - Rejected
        COM->>TEN: Clone and Modify Quotation
    else Client Requests Advisor
        CLI->>TEN: Request Advisor
        TEN->>COM: Notification - Advisor Requested
        COM->>CLI: Direct Contact
    end

diagrams.title: Route Types and Cost Source Applicability
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    ROUTE[Route Configuration] --> TYPE{Route Type}
    TYPE -->|Urban| URB[Urban Route]
    TYPE -->|National| NAT[National Route]
    TYPE -->|Container Return| CR[Container Return]
    
    URB --> URB_COST[Cost Sources]
    URB_COST --> URB_H[Historical Data Only]
    URB_COST --> URB_R[RNDC if Available]
    URB --> URB_NOTE[No CICETAC\nNo Cargo Manifest Required]
    
    NAT --> NAT_COST[Cost Sources]
    NAT_COST --> NAT_C[CICETAC Floor]
    NAT_COST --> NAT_R[RNDC Statistical]
    NAT_COST --> NAT_H[Historical Data]
    NAT --> NAT_NOTE[Full Manifest Required]
    
    CR --> CR_COST[Cost Sources]
    CR_COST --> CR_H[Historical Data]
    CR --> CR_TYPES{Return Type}
    CR_TYPES --> EXP[Express Return]
    CR_TYPES --> CONS[Consolidated Return]
    CR_TYPES --> PORT[Port Return]
    
    subgraph SAT[Additional Services - SAT]
        ITR[ITR - Inspection]
        EXTRA[Extra Delivery]
        STANDBY[Standby Charges]
        DEVOL[Container Return Service]
    end
